title: Transactional Outbox, Relay, Inbox, Sagas, and Compensation · Contract Lens: Dual Write Failure Matrix
summary: An HTTP transaction commits database state then publishes, or publishes then rolls back; a relay deletes outbox rows before confirms; a multi-service workflow treats retries as rollback. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dual write failure matrix increment in outbox, relay, inbox, and compensation protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Enumerate database-first, publish-first, timeout, crash, confirm-loss, rollback, and duplicate failure windows for a dual write.
Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Dual Write Failure Matrix · Theory Dual Write Failure Matrix
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 8696 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to close the local dual-write gap and preserve confirm or external-effect ambiguity; retain duplicate-safe identity through every ambiguous handoff; then introduce a sequence gap for one partition while another stays healthy. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Dual Write Failure Matrix contract for the supplied data.
